title: T-001 — Foundation extension: app.current_site_id() helper + withSiteContext HOF (§9.1, §11.4, §15.1)
prompt:
# T-001 — Foundation extension: app.current_site_id() + withSiteContext HOF

PRD: docs/prd/14-MULTI-SITE-PRD.md §9.1, §11.4 V-MS-15..17, §15.1 (current_site_id helper SECURITY DEFINER); foundation extension dependency on docs/prd/00-FOUNDATION-PRD.md §5 RLS, §8 multi-tenant
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira
Audit source: _meta/audits/2026-05-14-prd-vs-tasks-coverage-gaps.md §14-MULTI-SITE slice MS-001

## Goal
Extend the foundation tenant-context primitives (T-125 `withOrgContext`) with the parallel site-scoping layer required by all site-scoped RLS policies in §9.8. Add the `app.current_site_id()` Postgres SECURITY DEFINER helper, the `app.set_site_context(session_token uuid, site uuid)` setter trusted via `app.session_site_contexts`, and a `withSiteContext` HOF wrapping `withOrgContext` so every Server Action / route handler that touches a site-scoped table can enter a tx with both `org_id` and `site_id` GUCs primed before RLS evaluation.

This is a foundation extension task — it adds primitives to `packages/db` that downstream 14-multi-site tasks (T-002..T-030) and cross-module site-scoped consumers (05-WH, 08-PROD, 09-QA, 10-FIN, 11-SHIP, 12-REP, 13-MAINT, 15-OEE) depend on. Coordinate with 00-foundation: this task creates the GUC/helper alongside an extended set-context wrapper; the foundation manifest should be updated to reflect the cross-module carry-forward.

## Implementation contract
1. New migration `packages/db/migrations/0040_site_context.sql`:
   - `CREATE TABLE app.session_site_contexts (session_token uuid PRIMARY KEY, user_id uuid NOT NULL, org_id uuid NOT NULL, site_id uuid NULL, created_at timestamptz DEFAULT now())` — NULL site_id is the explicit "ALL sites" signal for super_admin per V-MS-07.
   - `CREATE FUNCTION app.set_site_context(session_token uuid, site uuid) RETURNS void LANGUAGE plpgsql SECURITY DEFINER` — verifies row exists in `app.session_site_contexts`, asserts `site_id IS NULL OR site = site_id` (else raise PG 28000), then `PERFORM set_config('app.current_site_id', site::text, true)`.
   - `CREATE FUNCTION app.current_site_id() RETURNS uuid LANGUAGE sql SECURITY DEFINER STABLE LEAKPROOF` — returns `NULLIF(current_setting('app.current_site_id', true), '')::uuid`. NULL = super_admin / ALL-sites mode.
   - Grants: `EXECUTE` only to `app_user`. Revoke `app_writer` from raw GUC SET.
2. New file `packages/db/src/with-site-context.ts` exports `withSiteContext<T>({ sessionToken, orgId, siteId, userId }, handler)` — composes on top of `withOrgContext` (T-125): opens tx, calls `app.set_org_context(sessionToken, orgId)`, then `app.set_site_context(sessionToken, siteId)`, sets `app.current_user_id`, runs handler. ROLLBACK on throw. PG 28000 surfaces as typed `SiteContextRejected`. When `siteId` is `null`, must explicitly call `app.set_site_context(sessionToken, NULL)` to declare ALL-sites mode (audit captured).
3. Next.js wrapper `apps/web/lib/auth/with-site-context-route.ts`: reads `sessionToken`, `orgId`, `siteId` from `x-site-id` header + session (V-MS-15 returns 400 SITE_CONTEXT_REQUIRED when user has multiple sites and header is absent; V-MS-16 returns 403 SITE_ACCESS_DENIED when header does not match `site_user_access`). Returns 401 if session missing; never acquires DB connection in that path.
4. Tests (vitest + testcontainers Postgres 16):
   - `packages/db/__tests__/with-site-context.test.ts`: two sites X and Y, app_user under each — site-scoped query returns only its own site rows; ALL-sites mode (siteId=null after super_admin row exists) returns both.
   - V-MS-16 negative: caller asserts siteId=Y while session_site_contexts has siteId=X → `SiteContextRejected` (PG 28000); tx rolled back.
   - `apps/web/lib/auth/__tests__/with-site-context-route.test.ts`: 400 SITE_CONTEXT_REQUIRED when multi-site user, no header; 403 SITE_ACCESS_DENIED when header mismatched; 200 happy path.
   - Source-grep: `with-site-context.ts` MUST NOT read `current_setting('app.current_site_id')` directly outside the helper file; must call `app.current_site_id()`.
5. Update foundation `_meta/atomic-tasks/00-foundation/coverage.md` under a new section `## Tenant-context extension — site_id (14-multi-site dependency T-001)` listing this cross-module carry-forward.

## Files
- packages/db/migrations/0040_site_context.sql [create]
- packages/db/src/with-site-context.ts [create]
- packages/db/__tests__/with-site-context.test.ts [create]
- apps/web/lib/auth/with-site-context-route.ts [create]
- apps/web/lib/auth/__tests__/with-site-context-route.test.ts [create]
- _meta/atomic-tasks/00-foundation/coverage.md [modify]

## Acceptance criteria
<!-- AC count consolidated from 6→4 by Fixer F14a 2026-05-14 (no coverage lost) -->
1. Given migration 0040 is applied, when pg_proc is inspected, then `app.set_site_context(uuid, uuid)` and `app.current_site_id()` exist with SECURITY DEFINER + (current_site_id LEAKPROOF STABLE) flags, and EXECUTE is granted only to app_user.
2. Given two sites X and Y with rows in `app.session_site_contexts` for the same user, when `withSiteContext({siteId:X}, fn)` and `withSiteContext({siteId:Y}, fn)` run interleaved as `app_user`, then each handler's SELECT against a site-scoped table sees only its own site rows (zero leakage).
3. Given a session_site_contexts row has site_id=X, when caller invokes `withSiteContext({siteId:Y, ...})`, then `SiteContextRejected` (PG 28000) is thrown before the handler executes and the tx is rolled back.
4. Given a multi-site user requests without `x-site-id` header, when route handler runs through `with-site-context-route.ts`, then 400 SITE_CONTEXT_REQUIRED is returned (V-MS-15) and no DB connection is acquired.
5. Given `x-site-id` header references a site not in caller's `site_user_access`, when handler runs, then 403 SITE_ACCESS_DENIED is returned (V-MS-16).
6. Given the helper source is inspected, when grep'd for `current_setting('app.current_site_id')`, then only the single read inside `app.current_site_id()` SQL function exists — TypeScript code MUST call the function, never the GUC.

## Test strategy
- RED: write `packages/db/__tests__/with-site-context.test.ts` + `apps/web/lib/auth/__tests__/with-site-context-route.test.ts` asserting all 6 ACs before the helper exists; tests connect as `app_user`, seed `session_site_contexts`, and assert interleaved-tx isolation.
- Run `pnpm --filter @monopilot/db test:integration -- with-site-context` + `pnpm --filter @monopilot/web vitest run apps/web/lib/auth/__tests__/with-site-context-route.test.ts`.

## Risk red lines
- Do not read `current_setting('app.current_site_id')` directly from TypeScript — always call `app.current_site_id()` so the SECURITY DEFINER + LEAKPROOF contract holds.
- Do not allow `app_user` to call `app.set_site_context` with an arbitrary `site` not present in its `session_site_contexts` row — PG 28000 must be raised by the setter, not by RLS.
- Do not let NULL site_id silently fall back to ALL-sites without an explicit super_admin row — the setter must distinguish 'no row' (reject) from 'row with site_id NULL' (allow).
- Do not couple this helper to a specific module; it is a foundation primitive consumed by 05-WH/08-PROD/09-QA/10-FIN/11-SHIP/12-REP/13-MAINT/15-OEE.
- Do not bypass `withOrgContext` — site context is layered on top, not in place of, org context.

pipeline_inputs.description: Foundation extension creating app.current_site_id() helper + app.set_site_context() setter trusted via app.session_site_contexts + withSiteContext HOF that composes on top of T-125 withOrgContext. All site-scoped RLS policies (§9.8 ~20 tables) consume this primitive.
pipeline_inputs.details: Migration 0040 adds `app.session_site_contexts(session_token, user_id, org_id, site_id NULLABLE for super_admin ALL mode)`, `app.set_site_context(session_token, site)` SECURITY DEFINER setter that verifies the row and raises PG 28000 on mismatch, and `app.current_site_id()` SECURITY DEFINER STABLE LEAKPROOF reader. TypeScript `withSiteContext` composes on `withOrgContext` (T-125) — opens tx, sets org context, sets site context, sets actor GUC, runs handler, rolls back on throw, PG 28000 → typed SiteContextRejected. Next.js wrapper enforces V-MS-15 (400 SITE_CONTEXT_REQUIRED) and V-MS-16 (403 SITE_ACCESS_DENIED). Tests prove interleaved-tx isolation across two sites + setter rejection on mismatch + source-grep ban on raw GUC reads. Updates foundation coverage.md to register this carry-forward. AC count consolidated from 6→4 by Fixer F14a 2026-05-14 (no coverage lost)
out_of_scope: Per-table ALTER TABLE adding site_id columns — tracked in T-030 (activation migration).
Per-Server-Action retrofit of every existing actions/*.ts — module-level work tracked separately.
RLS policy variants for individual tables — generated by the rule in T-006.
x-site-id middleware Next.js header injection — T-007.

cross_module_dependencies.reason: withSiteContext composes on top of T-125 withOrgContext HOF; this task is a registered carry-forward extension to 00-foundation primitives.
Re-uses app.set_org_context setter pattern + session_org_contexts trust table; the new app.session_site_contexts table mirrors that contract.

acceptance_criteria: Given migration 0040 is applied, when pg_proc is inspected, then app.set_site_context(uuid, uuid) and app.current_site_id() exist with SECURITY DEFINER + (current_site_id LEAKPROOF STABLE) flags, and EXECUTE is granted only to app_user.

Given two sites X and Y with rows in app.session_site_contexts for the same user, when withSiteContext({siteId:X}) and withSiteContext({siteId:Y}) run interleaved as app_user, then each handler's SELECT against a site-scoped table sees only its own site rows (zero leakage).
Given a session_site_contexts row has site_id=X, when caller invokes withSiteContext({siteId:Y}), then SiteContextRejected (PG 28000) is thrown before the handler executes and the tx is rolled back.

Given a multi-site user requests without x-site-id header, when route handler runs through with-site-context-route.ts, then 400 SITE_CONTEXT_REQUIRED is returned (V-MS-15) and no DB connection is acquired; AND given x-site-id header references a site not in caller's site_user_access, when handler runs, then 403 SITE_ACCESS_DENIED (V-MS-16) is returned.
test_strategy: RED: write packages/db/__tests__/with-site-context.test.ts asserting interleaved-tx isolation + PG 28000 rejection + source-grep before the helper exists.
Verify source-grep: only a single current_setting('app.current_site_id') read inside app.current_site_id() SQL function; TypeScript code must call the function, never the GUC (original AC6).
risk_red_lines: Do not read current_setting('app.current_site_id') directly from TypeScript — always call app.current_site_id() so SECURITY DEFINER + LEAKPROOF holds.
Do not allow app_user to call app.set_site_context with an arbitrary site not in session_site_contexts — PG 28000 must come from the setter, not RLS.
Do not let NULL site_id silently fall back to ALL-sites without explicit super_admin row — distinguish 'no row' (reject) from 'row with site_id NULL' (allow).
Do not couple this helper to a specific module; it is a foundation primitive shared across 8 modules.
Do not bypass withOrgContext — site context layers on top, not in place of, org context.
